vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1617: Substrate: work agents spawn but kickoff prompt is never delivered — idle 'ghost' agents at the launch screen, misreported healthy

plan.title: Substrate: work agents spawn but kickoff prompt is never delivered — idle 'ghost' agents, misreported healthy
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-8

narratives.Problem: A work agent can spawn (tmux up, Claude running, state status=running role=work) but never receive its kickoff/work prompt, sitting at the pristine launch screen forever. Spawn delivery is fire-and-forget: when waitForReadySignal returns false the code only console.errors then markAgentRunning anyway, and deliverAgentMessage returns void and silently falls through to an unverifiable tmux sendKeys. The health classifier (determineHealthStatus) keys off lastActivity, which the heartbeat keeps refreshing, so the idle ghost is reported 'healthy'. When the deacon later orphans→stops→auto-resumes such an agent, resumeAgent does `--resume <sessionId>` into a session that never received a kickoff and sends only a generic continue. Result: silent zero-progress stalls masked as healthy, defeating autonomous operation.
narratives.Proposal: Introduce a tri-state kickoffDelivered flag on AgentState (undefined=legacy, false=spawned-not-confirmed, true=confirmed). Make deliverAgentMessage report a structured DeliveryResult so spawn can verify which transport carried the kickoff. At work-agent spawn, set kickoffDelivered=false up front, verify delivery (ready signal + transport ack), retry once, and escalate (recordAgentFailure) instead of silently marking running on failure. Add an early stall rule to determineHealthStatus that flags role=work/status=running/kickoffDelivered=false past a 5-min grace as 'stalled' (independent of the heartbeat-masked lastActivity). On deacon resume of a work agent with kickoffDelivered=false, re-deliver the persisted initial-prompt.md kickoff (idempotently) rather than relaunching the TUI with a bare continue. Cover the full ghost lifecycle with unit and integration tests.

autoDecisions.summary: Tri-state kickoffDelivered field on AgentState rather than a new sidecar file
autoDecisions.rationale: determineHealthStatus already reads state.json directly; co-locating the flag needs zero extra I/O and matches existing lifecycle flags (stoppedByUser, paused, troubled).

autoDecisions.summary: Stall rule fires only on kickoffDelivered===false, never undefined
autoDecisions.rationale: Agents running before this ships have undefined; excluding undefined prevents false-positives on legitimate in-flight legacy agents.

autoDecisions.summary: 5-minute grace from startedAt before flagging stalled
autoDecisions.rationale: Cold model/pi/codex warmup can take 30-60s+; 5 min sits comfortably above the 30s ready-poll + one retry while still catching real hour-long ghosts.
Lets the flywheel/dashboard target the ghost failure mode specifically; the issue explicitly calls for classifying these as stalled, not healthy.
Both spawn paths already persist the kickoff there; re-reading avoids re-running buildWorkAgentPrompt and keeps the resume fix self-contained.
Backward-compatible — existing void-ignoring callers are unaffected; gives spawn the supervisor success/failure signal the issue requires.

items.title: Add tri-state kickoffDelivered field to AgentState

narrative.Action: Add `kickoffDelivered?: boolean` to the AgentState interface in src/lib/agents.ts with a doc comment defining the tri-state contract (undefined = legacy/pre-feature or non-applicable; false = spawned but kickoff not yet confirmed; true = kickoff confirmed delivered). Add the passthrough in cleanAgentState() so the field is persisted and round-trips through saveAgentState/getAgentState. No behavioral change in this bead — only the schema and its documented semantics.

subItems.title: AgentState includes kickoffDelivered?: boolean with a doc comment specifying undefined/false/true semantics
cleanAgentState() carries kickoffDelivered through so it persists in state.json and survives a save/load round-trip
npm run typecheck passes with the new field

narrative.Action: Change deliverAgentMessage's return type from Promise<void> to Promise<DeliveryResult> where DeliveryResult = { ok: boolean; path: 'supervisor'|'channels'|'tmux'|'pi'|'codex'; failure?: string }. Return the appropriate result at every exit (supervisor success => {ok:true,path:'supervisor'}; channels success => channels; auto-mode tmux fallback => {ok:true,path:'tmux'} since sendKeys did run; codex => codex). Strict-mode explicit 'supervisor'/'channels' still THROW on failure (unchanged). Change is additive — existing callers that ignore the return value are unaffected. Update src/lib/__tests__/deliver-agent-message.test.ts to assert the returned result shape per path.

subItems.title: deliverAgentMessage returns DeliveryResult with correct {ok,path} for supervisor, channels, and tmux-fallback auto-mode paths
Strict 'supervisor'/'channels' delivery still throws MessageDeliveryFailed on failure (existing behavior preserved)
Existing callers (tells, feedback, resume) continue to compile and behave unchanged; deliver-agent-message.test.ts asserts the new return shape and passes

items.title: Verify, persist, retry and escalate kickoff delivery at work-agent spawn

narrative.Action: In both work-agent spawn paths — spawnAgent (work) and spawnRun when role==='work' in src/lib/agents.ts — make kickoff delivery verified instead of fire-and-forget. Set state.kickoffDelivered=false before delivery and save. Ensure the kickoff is persisted to <agentDir>/initial-prompt.md for later resume re-delivery. After waitForReadySignal: if ready, deliver and inspect the DeliveryResult; on a confirmed path set state.kickoffDelivered=true and save. On ready=false OR an unverified delivery, retry the ready-wait + delivery ONCE. If it still fails, do NOT silently markAgentRunning as a healthy running agent — call recordAgentFailure with reason 'kickoff delivery failed' and emit an activity-level error so the failure surfaces. Set kickoffDelivered=true on the Pi (writePiAgentPrompt) and Codex (inline exec) delivery paths too so those harnesses are never falsely flagged (hazard H5). Per hazard H3, treat tmux-only ok as weak: confirm via the ready signal, and only flip kickoffDelivered=true when the agent reached the prompt and a delivery was issued.

subItems.title: spawnAgent and spawnRun(work) set kickoffDelivered=false before delivery and flip it to true only after a verified delivery (ready signal observed + delivery issued / supervisor ack)

On ready=false or failed delivery, the spawn retries once, then on continued failure records an agent failure (recordAgentFailure, reason 'kickoff delivery failed') and emits an error activity entry instead of marking the agent healthy-running with no kickoff
The kickoff prompt is persisted to <agentDir>/initial-prompt.md for work agents in both spawn paths (enabling resume re-delivery)
Pi and Codex spawn paths set kickoffDelivered=true after their own delivery step so they are not falsely classified stalled
Unit test added asserting: successful delivery => kickoffDelivered=true; ready=false twice => failure recorded and kickoffDelivered stays false

items.title: Classify kickoff-stalled work agents as 'stalled' in determineHealthStatus

narrative.Action: Add an early stall rule to determineHealthStatus in src/dashboard/lib/health-filtering.ts, placed BEFORE the lastActivity-based staleness logic and AFTER the liveness (alive) check. When state.role==='work' && agentStatus==='running' && state.kickoffDelivered===false && (Date.now() - Date.parse(state.startedAt)) > 5*60*1000, return { status: 'stalled', reason: 'Work agent running with no kickoff delivered since spawn' }. The rule must NOT consult lastActivity (the heartbeat masks it — that is the root cause of the misreport). Only fire on explicit ===false, never undefined (hazard H1). Verify the /api/health/agents consumer (src/dashboard/server/routes/misc.ts) and any frontend status switch tolerate the new 'stalled' value; map it to an appropriate severity if a strict union exists (hazard H6).

subItems.title: determineHealthStatus returns { status: 'stalled' } for role=work, status=running, kickoffDelivered=false, startedAt older than the 5-min grace
kickoffDelivered=true OR undefined never produces 'stalled'; an agent within the 5-min grace is not flagged
The rule does not read lastActivity (so a fresh heartbeat cannot mask the stall)
New unit test src/dashboard/lib/__tests__/health-filtering.test.ts covers stalled-positive, within-grace-negative, kickoffDelivered=true-negative, and undefined-negative cases
The /api/health/agents route and frontend tolerate the 'stalled' status (no crash / strict-union rejection)

items.title: Re-deliver kickoff on deacon/agent resume when kickoffDelivered is false

narrative.Action: Make work-agent resume re-establish kickoff context for ghosts. In resumeAgent (and the fallback-restart path) in src/lib/agents.ts: when the agent is role==='work' and state.kickoffDelivered===false, after the session is ready, re-deliver the original kickoff read from <agentDir>/initial-prompt.md (instead of, or prepended to, the generic continue prompt), then set state.kickoffDelivered=true on confirmed re-delivery. Make it idempotent: kickoffDelivered===true/undefined keeps today's generic-continue behavior (hazard H4). This closes the autoResumeStoppedWorkAgents → resumeAgent path that the deacon uses after recoverOrphanedAgents marks a ghost running→stopped, so a re-launched ghost actually receives its task rather than a bare TUI + 'continue'.

subItems.title: Resuming a role=work agent with kickoffDelivered=false re-delivers the kickoff from <agentDir>/initial-prompt.md after the ready signal
kickoffDelivered is set to true after a confirmed re-delivery on resume
Resume of an agent with kickoffDelivered=true or undefined is unchanged (no double-delivery) — idempotent
If initial-prompt.md is missing for a kickoffDelivered=false work agent, resume logs/surfaces the gap rather than silently sending a contextless continue
Unit test added covering re-delivery on kickoffDelivered=false and no-op on true

items.title: Integration test: full ghost-agent lifecycle (spawn-fail → stalled → resume re-delivers)

narrative.Action: Add an integration-style test that exercises the end-to-end ghost failure mode against the real (mocked-transport) seam: (1) a work agent whose kickoff delivery is forced to fail ends with kickoffDelivered=false and a recorded failure rather than a silent healthy-running state; (2) determineHealthStatus classifies that state.json as 'stalled'; (3) a resume of that agent re-delivers from initial-prompt.md and flips kickoffDelivered=true, after which determineHealthStatus no longer returns 'stalled'. Use fake timers for any grace/backoff waits per the repo's fake-timer rule; do not rely on real wall-clock delays.

subItems.title: Test reproduces: forced kickoff-delivery failure leaves kickoffDelivered=false + recorded failure (no healthy-running ghost)
Test asserts determineHealthStatus returns 'stalled' for that state, then 'healthy' (or no longer 'stalled') after resume re-delivery flips kickoffDelivered=true
Test uses vi.useFakeTimers / advanceTimersByTimeAsync for the grace window — no real setTimeout waits
npm test, npm run typecheck, and npm run lint all pass

review.notes: Agent re-review request (1/25): Fixed lint and test

history.notes: Agent re-review request (1/25): Fixed lint and test
